skill.name: documentation-writer
skill.description: A Diataxis-focused technical writing skill that helps plan and draft tutorials, how-to guides, reference material, and explanations.
skill.summary: Create structured software documentation using the Diataxis framework.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged one hardcoded URL and four weak cryptography patterns. Review found the URL is a documentation framework reference, and the weak cryptography matches are false positives from normal prose, not code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, script execution, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Framework URL in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references https://diataxis.fr/ as the source framework for the writing method. This is a static network indicator, but the skill contains no code that opens the URL or sends data to it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is visible in plain instructional text and is not part of an executable command or request. The surrounding instructions also restrict external source use unless the user provides a link and asks for it.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography matches occur inside ordinary documentation prose, such as description-related words and Diataxis document type descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, encryption API, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain writing instructions and natural language only. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or any other weak cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Software teams often mix tutorials, how-to guides, reference pages, and explanations into unclear documents. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan and draft focused Markdown documentation using the Diataxis framework.
actual_capabilities: Classifies documentation requests into tutorials, how-to guides, reference pages, or explanations.
Asks for document type, target audience, user goal, and scope before drafting.
Proposes a structured outline before writing the full document.
Writes final documentation in well-formatted Markdown after outline approval.
Uses provided Markdown files as style and terminology context without copying them by default.
limitations: Requires enough project context to write accurate technical content.
Does not consult external websites unless the user provides a link and instructs it to do so.
Waits for outline approval before drafting full documentation.
Does not replace technical review by maintainers or subject matter experts.

use_cases.title: Create onboarding tutorials
use_cases.description: Turn a setup flow into a learning-oriented tutorial with clear steps, expected results, and a defined audience.

use_cases.title: Improve task documentation
use_cases.description: Convert scattered notes into a focused how-to guide that solves one operational or development problem.

use_cases.title: Structure reference material
use_cases.description: Plan API, configuration, or command reference pages with consistent sections and concise descriptions.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the document type
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for help classifying a documentation idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right Diataxis document type for this topic: [topic]. Ask me for the target audience, user goal, and scope before recommending a structure.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a tutorial outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a learning-oriented document plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Diataxis tutorial outline for [task or feature]. The audience is [audience], and the desired learning outcome is [outcome]. Include sections and brief notes for each section.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a how-to guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to improve existing task documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft as a Diataxis how-to guide: [paste draft]. Identify unclear scope, missing prerequisites, and steps that should be more direct. Then propose a revised outline.

prompt_templates.title: Build a documentation set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple coordinated documents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a small Diataxis documentation set for [product or feature]. Include one tutorial, two how-to guides, one reference page, and one explanation. Define audience, goal, scope, and dependencies for each document.

output_examples.input: I need documentation for installing our CLI.
output_examples.output: The skill would first ask whether the goal is learning, solving a task, reference lookup, or conceptual understanding. It would then propose a tutorial or how-to outline based on the answer.

output_examples.input: Here is a draft API reference page. Make it clearer.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify missing reference sections, suggest consistent headings, and keep explanations separate from parameter descriptions.

output_examples.input: Create docs for a new authentication feature.
output_examples.output: The skill would propose separate Diataxis documents, such as a tutorial for first setup, how-to guides for common tasks, reference material for settings, and an explanation of authentication concepts.

best_practices: Provide the audience, user goal, and product context before asking for a draft.
Approve or revise the proposed outline before requesting full content.
Share existing Markdown files when tone, terminology, or structure must match current documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for one document to be a tutorial, reference page, and explanation at the same time.
Do not request technical claims without providing source material or product details.
Do not skip outline review when the document scope is large or ambiguous.

faq.question: What framework does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the Diataxis framework, which separates documentation into tutorials, how-to guides, reference, and explanation.

faq.question: Can it write full Markdown documents?
faq.answer: Yes. It proposes an outline first, then writes the full Markdown document after approval.

faq.question: Does it browse external websites?
faq.answer: No. The skill says external websites should only be used when the user provides a link and instructs the assistant to use it.

faq.question: Can it improve existing documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use provided Markdown files as context for tone, terminology, structure, and scope.

faq.question: Is it only for software teams?
faq.answer: It is designed for software documentation, but the document planning approach can help other technical domains.

faq.question: Does it verify technical accuracy?
faq.answer: It aims for accurate writing, but users should provide reliable source material and review the final content.
